title: Dryopteris oligodictyos
intro: Dryopteris oligodictyos, also known as Japanese lady fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The bioactive constituents of Japanese lady fern include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Dryopteris oligodictyos is often prepared as an infusion or tincture, and is used to make teas, capsules, and other supplements that are used to promote overall health and well-being.
taxonomy: Dryopteris oligodictyos is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It is native to tropical and subtropical regions of Asia and the Pacific Islands. The classification of Dryopteris oligodictyos includes its botanical name, which is a binomial nomenclature consisting of the genus name Dryopteris and the species name oligodictyos. This species of fern belongs to the kingdom Plantae and is a member of the phylum Pteridophyta, which includes all ferns and fern allies. Further classification includes the order Polypodiales, the subclass Polypodiidae, and the class Polypodiopsida.
